---
title: "Teaching"
---

## Teaching Philosophy
My teaching approach emphasizes active learning, practical application, and the integration of real-world data into the classroom. Drawing on my research in climate-driven mosquito population dynamics, I aim to equip students with the analytical skills and problem-solving strategies needed to address complex questions in mathematics, biology, and data science. I place particular emphasis on helping students connect mathematical theory with computational implementation and real-world applications.

## Teaching Experience
I have taught and assisted in undergraduate mathematics and biology courses, including:

- Differential Equations  
- Basic and Applied Statistics  
- R Programming for Biological Sciences  
- Mathematical Methods  

## Instructional Skills
I have experience in:

- Developing instructional materials and quantitative training resources tailored to diverse learning needs  
- Assessing student performance through assignments, projects, and exams  
- Supporting students in R programming, data analysis, and computational modeling  
- Mentoring students in research design, modeling, and scientific communication
